lifecycle.heading: One Partner. Every Financial Stage.
lifecycle.subtitle: We believe financial wellbeing isn't a one-time event — it's a journey. Our lifecycle approach means we're with you at every stage, not just when markets are exciting.

hero.subtext: From understanding money to investing wisely, protecting your family, and recovering lost assets — Saarthi Finance walks with you through every stage of your financial journey.

services.heading: What We Help You With
services.subheading: Each service is designed to address a distinct phase of your financial life — independently or as a connected whole.

serviceCards.title: Financial Education
serviceCards.description: Workshops and curated resources to help you understand financial products, market mechanisms, and goal-setting before making any investment decision.

serviceCards.title: Investment Advisory
serviceCards.description: Goal-aligned market insights on mutual funds and other instruments. Structured facilitation aligned with your life goals — not market timing.

serviceCards.title: Insurance Advisory
serviceCards.description: Unbiased assessment of your life, health, and critical illness insurance needs. We help you protect what matters — without over-insuring or under-insuring.

serviceCards.title: Recover & Regularise
serviceCards.description: Specialist assistance to reclaim shares and dividends transferred to IEPF, dematerialise physical share certificates, and resolve transmission issues.

whyUs.heading: Insights You Can Trust. Built on Education.
whyUs.description: The financial services industry can be overwhelming — full of jargon, conflicts of interest, and products designed for commissions rather than your needs. We built Saarthi Finance to be different: transparent, fee-based, and genuinely family-oriented.

whyChooseUs.title: Conflict-Free Guidance
whyChooseUs.description: We operate on education-first principles — not commissions on products sold. Your interest is our only priority.

whyChooseUs.title: Education Before Action
whyChooseUs.description: We believe informed clients make better decisions. Every recommendation comes with a clear explanation of the why.

whyChooseUs.title: Family-Centred Approach
whyChooseUs.description: Financial planning isn't individual — it affects families. We consider succession, protection, and long-term wellbeing holistically.

whyChooseUs.title: Transparent Operations
whyChooseUs.description: We operate with full transparency and integrity within applicable frameworks so you can focus on your goals — not paperwork.

process.title: How We Work Together
process.subtitle: A structured, transparent process with no surprises — from first conversation to long-term support.

steps.title: Understand
steps.description: We start by listening. A structured discovery session to understand your financial situation, family goals, and current challenges — free of any product pitch.

steps.title: Plan
steps.description: A personalised financial plan covering investments, insurance, and recovery priorities — aligned with your income, risk appetite, and life stage.

steps.title: Execute
steps.description: Step-by-step implementation with complete documentation. You retain full control; we handle the complexity of regulatory paperwork and process.

steps.title: Support
steps.description: Ongoing quarterly reviews, access to updated educational resources, and a dedicated relationship manager for your evolving needs.

articles.heading: Educational Resources
articles.subheading: Practical guides written for Indian families — no jargon, no hype.

featuredArticles.title: How to Claim Your Family's Shares from IEPF — A Step-by-Step Guide
featuredArticles.excerpt: Millions of rupees in unclaimed dividends and shares sit with the Investor Education and Protection Fund. If your family has shares that stopped receiving dividends, they may have been transferred to IEPF. Here's how to reclaim them.

featuredArticles.title: Why Every Indian Family Needs Term Insurance — And How to Pick the Right One
featuredArticles.excerpt: Term insurance is the simplest, most affordable financial protection you can give your family. But choosing the wrong cover amount, tenure, or insurer can leave gaps. Here's what matters.

featuredArticles.title: Dematerialising Physical Share Certificates: What Families Need to Know
featuredArticles.excerpt: Physical share certificates are valid but increasingly difficult to trade or transfer. Demat conversion is now essential for transfers. Here’s a clear guide to converting your old paper certificates.

testimonials.heading: Families We've Had the Privilege to Serve
testimonials.subheading: Their words reflect our commitment — not promises of returns, but clarity, trust, and outcomes.
testimonials.disclaimer: Testimonials reflect individual experiences. Results may vary. This is not an endorsement of investment returns.

items.quote: Saarthi helped us reclaim shares that had been stuck in physical certificate form for over 20 years after my father passed. The team handled everything — from demat to IEPF — with immense patience and transparency.
items.name: Geeta Sharma

items.quote: I was confused between various insurance products being pushed at me. The adviser at Saarthi took time to explain what I actually need vs what I was being sold. No commission bias — just honest guidance.
items.name: Rohit Menon

items.quote: Their financial education sessions made me realise I didn't need to be afraid of investing. Starting with the basics and understanding risk properly changed everything for us as a family.
items.name: Priya Nair

footer.designedFor: Designed for Indian families. Committed to

hero.headline: Understand Money
hero.subtext: Curated, jargon-free financial education for Indian families. Build a strong foundation before making any decision — from mutual funds to insurance to estate planning.

learn.whyPara1: Most financial mistakes aren't made out of greed — they're made out of lack of understanding. A product sold without explanation, a risk level misunderstood, or an insurance policy bought under pressure.
learn.whyPara2: At Saarthi Finance, we believe every advisory relationship should start with education. Before we recommend anything, we want you to understand what it is, why it might suit you, and what the risks are.
learn.whyPara3: Our educational resources are written specifically for Indian families — not adapted from American or British finance content. We cover Indian regulations, Indian tax laws, and Indian financial products.
learn.topicsSubheading: Organised by complexity level so you can start where you are and build from there.

educationTopics.title: Basics of Financial Planning
educationTopics.description: Understand the fundamentals: budgeting, emergency funds, goal-setting, and the difference between saving and investing.

educationTopics.title: Understanding Mutual Funds
educationTopics.description: How mutual funds work, different fund categories, NAV, expense ratios, SIPs vs lump sums — everything you need before you invest.

educationTopics.title: Insurance Fundamentals
educationTopics.description: Term life, health, critical illness, and ULIPs explained. Learn how to calculate the right coverage and avoid common traps.

educationTopics.title: Tax & Investment Interaction
educationTopics.description: How LTCG, STCG, Section 80C, 80D, and ELSS work — and how to plan your investments to be tax-efficient without distorting your portfolio.

educationTopics.title: Real Estate vs Financial Assets
educationTopics.description: A balanced, data-led comparison between property and financial investments. Understanding liquidity, yield, and concentration risk.

educationTopics.title: Family Financial Planning
educationTopics.description: How to plan finances as a family unit — joint goals, succession planning, inheritance, and protecting dependants at every life stage.

educationTopics.title: Wills, Nominations & Succession
educationTopics.description: Why nominations matter, how to write a will, and how to ensure your assets are transferred smoothly to your loved ones.

educationTopics.title: Portfolio Review & Rebalancing
educationTopics.description: When and how to review your portfolio, what rebalancing means, and why staying the course during market volatility is usually the right answer.

learn.featuredSubheading: Our most-read guides for families new to structured financial planning.

featuredArticles.title: Goal-Based Investing: Why Your Portfolio Should Have a Purpose
featuredArticles.excerpt: Investing without a goal is like driving without a destination. Here's how to build an investment portfolio around your actual life goals — child's education, retirement, home purchase.

featuredArticles.title: 5 Common SIP Myths That Could Cost Indian Families Money
featuredArticles.excerpt: SIPs are powerful tools, but misconceptions about how they work lead to poor decisions. We address the five most common myths with clear explanations.

featuredArticles.title: Building Your Emergency Fund: How Much is Enough?
featuredArticles.excerpt: An emergency fund is the most important financial tool most families overlook. Learn how to size yours correctly and where to keep it for safety and accessibility.

learn.faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about our learning resources.

faqItems.question: Do I need to be financially literate before booking a consultation?
faqItems.answer: Not at all. Our educational resources and consultations are designed to meet you where you are. Whether you're starting from zero or looking to deepen your knowledge, we tailor every interaction to your current understanding.

faqItems.question: Are your educational resources free?
faqItems.answer: Yes. All articles, guides, and educational content on this website are completely free. We believe financial education should be accessible to every Indian family, not gated behind a paywall.

faqItems.question: How is this different from reading a personal finance blog?
faqItems.answer: Our content is curated by SEBI-registered advisers for the Indian regulatory and market context — not generic advice copied from international sources. Everything is reviewed for accuracy and compliance. We also offer follow-up consultations if you want personalised guidance after reading.

faqItems.question: What topics do your financial education sessions cover?
faqItems.answer: We cover goal-setting, budgeting, mutual fund basics, insurance fundamentals, tax-efficient investing, estate planning, and more. Upcoming workshop topics are listed in our Resources section.

learn.ctaTitle: Ready to Move from Learning to Action?
learn.ctaSubtitle: Once you feel confident in the basics, book a free consultation and we'll build a personalised plan together.

hero.headline: Goal-Based Investing,
hero.subtext: Structured, conflict-free investment advisory for Indian families. We build plans around your life — your goals, your timeline, your values — not market noise or product commissions.

problem.heading: The Problem with How Most People Invest
problem.para1: Most Indians invest reactively — responding to market rallies, friend recommendations, or a persuasive distributor. Products are bought without understanding them; portfolios grow messy and directionless; and at the end, it's unclear whether you're actually on track for any real goal.
problem.para2: Saarthi Finance brings structure to this chaos. We start with your goals — not with products. Every recommendation follows from a clear understanding of what you need, when you need it, and how much risk is genuinely appropriate for you.

items.title: Goal-Based Financial Planning
items.description: Your investments are organised around specific goals — child education, retirement corpus, emergency fund — with target dates and amounts. Not abstract 'wealth creation'.

items.title: Fund Facilitation
items.description: Goal-aligned facilitation on mutual funds. Fund selection is based on consistency, expense ratios, fund house quality, and your specific needs.

items.title: Fee-Only, No-Commission
items.description: We earn fees from clients — not commissions from fund houses or insurers. This eliminates conflicts of interest and ensures every recommendation serves you.

items.title: Portfolio Review & Rebalancing
items.description: Annual or quarterly reviews to ensure your portfolio stays aligned with your goals. Rebalancing is triggered by life events and allocation drift — not market timing.

items.title: Asset Allocation Strategy
items.description: Holistic allocation across equity, debt, gold, and cash — considering your existing real estate, EPF, PPF, and NPS holdings to avoid over-concentration.

items.title: Family Financial Planning
items.description: We plan for the family as a whole — joint goals, spouse's portfolio integration, minor children's education goals, and succession considerations.

whoItsFor.heading: Who This Service Is For
whoItsFor.subheading: Our advisory is designed for families at different life stages, not a one-size-fits-all product.

items.description: Starting your investment journey, wanting structure, clear goals, and a disciplined SIP-based approach. We help you avoid common early mistakes.
Balancing children's education funding, home loan, insurance, and retirement simultaneously. We build an integrated plan that doesn't compromise one goal for another.
Transitioning from accumulation to preservation. We help you de-risk strategically while ensuring your corpus lasts through retirement.
Already investing but unsure if you're on track, have too many funds, or bought products without understanding them. We audit and reorganise.

process.title: Our Market Insights Process
process.subtitle: A structured, five-step process from understanding your goals to ongoing support.

steps.title: Goals & Discovery Session
steps.description: A structured 45-minute conversation to map your income, expenses, dependants, existing investments, and key life goals such as retirement, education, or home purchase.

steps.title: Risk Profiling
steps.description: A validated psychometric questionnaire combined with a financial capacity analysis determines your risk profile — from conservative to aggressive — ensuring you never take more risk than you can afford or emotionally handle.

steps.title: Investment Plan Preparation
steps.description: A comprehensive written investment policy statement (IPS) detailing your goal timelines, asset allocation, recommended mutual funds, SIP amounts, and review triggers.

steps.title: Implementation Support
steps.description: Guidance on execution — opening accounts, setting up SIPs, consolidating existing folios. You execute; we guide and verify. No third-party intermediation.

steps.title: Quarterly Review
steps.description: Formal portfolio reviews every quarter with a written report comparing performance against your goals — not benchmarks. Rebalancing recommendations when drift exceeds thresholds.

faq.title: Market Insights — Your Questions Answered
faq.subtitle: Transparency is our policy. Here are answers to the questions we hear most often.

items.question: What kind of financial platform is Saarthi Finance?
items.answer: Saarthi Finance is a financial education and facilitation platform. We are not a SEBI-registered Investment Adviser or Portfolio Manager. We provide market insights, educational content, and asset facilitation services.

items.question: What is the difference between a financial educator and a mutual fund distributor?
items.answer: A financial educator helps you understand financial products and build informed decision-making skills, without earning commissions. A mutual fund distributor earns commissions from fund houses for selling products. At Saarthi Finance, we operate as an education-first platform — helping you understand options without pushing products.

items.question: Do you give advice on stocks or direct equity?
items.answer: Our advisory focuses on mutual funds as the primary investment vehicle for most retail investors. We do not provide stock-specific tips, targets, or trading recommendations. For direct equity exposure, we discuss index funds and ETFs where appropriate.

items.question: How much do your investment advisory services cost?
items.answer: We operate on a transparent fee structure for facilitation services. Fees are disclosed in full before any engagement. There are no hidden charges or commissions.

items.question: Can you help me review my existing investments?
items.answer: Absolutely. A portfolio review is one of our most common services. We assess your existing mutual funds, insurance-cum-investment products, and other holdings — provide a clear picture of redundancies, cost inefficiencies, and gaps — and recommend a consolidated, goal-aligned portfolio.

items.question: Do you promise any minimum returns?
items.answer: No. We do not promise, guarantee, or forecast specific investment returns. Investment in securities markets is subject to market risks. Our role is to ensure your investments are structured correctly for your goals and risk profile — outcomes depend on market performance over time.

cta.title: Let's Build an Investment Plan for Your Family
cta.subtitle: No products pitched. No returns promised. Just a clear conversation about your goals and how to reach them.

invest.educationalDisclaimer: Educational Disclosure: All market insights are for informational purposes only. Investment in securities market is subject to market risks. Past performance is not indicative of future returns.

hero.headline: Protect What Matters Most —
hero.subtext: Honest, unbiased insurance advisory for Indian families. We assess your protection needs independently and help you choose the right cover — without being influenced by commission structures.

problem.heading: Most Indian Families Are Either Over-Insured or Under-Protected
problem.para1: India has one of the lowest insurance penetration rates in the world, yet paradoxically, millions of families pay premiums into products they don't understand — ULIPs, endowment plans, and money-back policies sold as "investment plus insurance."
problem.para2: The result: premium outflow without adequate life cover, and investment returns lower than even simple fixed deposits. Our advisory starts with a clear insurance needs analysis — what you actually need, what you have, and the gap between the two.

insuranceTypes.heading: Insurance Advisory We Provide
insuranceTypes.subheading: Each type of cover serves a distinct purpose. We help you understand and obtain what you actually need.

items.title: Term Life Insurance
items.description: Pure protection for your family. We help you calculate the right sum assured based on your income replacement needs, outstanding liabilities, and dependant support requirements — not just a thumb-rule multiple.
keyPoints: Rider evaluation (waiver of premium, accidental death benefit)

items.title: Health Insurance
items.description: Medical costs are one of the biggest financial shocks families face. We help you choose comprehensive health cover with the right sum insured, the right features, and portability considerations.

items.title: Critical Illness Cover
items.description: A lump sum payment on diagnosis of serious illnesses like cancer, heart attack, or stroke — allowing you to focus on recovery without financial stress. Often overlooked, always essential.

items.title: Insurance Review for Seniors
items.description: Existing policyholders who've accumulated multiple policies over decades often have overlapping, inadequate, or misunderstood cover. We audit and rationalise.

items.title: Newly married couples
items.text: Starting a family requires term life review and health cover planning from the outset.

items.title: Parents with young children
items.text: Your cover must support your children's upbringing and education in your absence.

items.title: Home loan borrowers
items.text: Your sum assured must at minimum cover your outstanding home loan principal.

items.title: Families with ageing parents
items.text: Senior health cover planning requires specialist knowledge of available products.

items.title: Existing policyholders
items.text: If you've had policies for years but aren't sure what they cover — you need a review.

underInsuredBox.heading: The Cost of Being Under-Insured
underInsuredBox.para1: The average Indian breadwinner is insured for approximately ₹5–10 lakhs — enough to cover medical emergencies but nowhere near enough to replace income for a family of four for even three years.
underInsuredBox.para2: A 35-year-old earning ₹12 lakh per annum needs approximately ₹1.5–2 crore in term cover. The premium? Often less than ₹15,000 per year.

process.title: Our Insurance Advisory Process
process.subtitle: From needs assessment to annual review — a complete advisory lifecycle for your protection.

steps.title: Insurance Needs Analysis
steps.description: We review your income, dependants, outstanding loans, existing policies, and future obligations to calculate exactly what protection gaps exist.

steps.title: Market Comparison
steps.description: We compare products from leading IRDAI-approved insurers on premium, claim settlement ratio, features, and exclusions — not on commission.

steps.title: Recommendation & Disclosure
steps.description: A written recommendation with full disclosure of our POSP code, insurer, premium, and our rationale. You decide — we explain.

steps.title: Application Support
steps.description: Assistance with proposal form accuracy, medical test guidance, and correct disclosure of health history — critical for claim settlement.

steps.title: Annual Review
steps.description: Annual check on whether your cover still matches your needs — especially after major life events like marriage, a child, a home loan, or job change.

items.question: How are you different from an insurance agent?
items.answer: A traditional insurance agent is typically tied to one or a few insurers and earns commissions from them. As a POSP (Point of Salesperson), we can offer products from multiple insurers and, crucially, our primary relationship and advice is guided by your needs. We combine POSP facilitation with independent advisory analysis.

items.question: Should I consider ULIPs for investment and insurance?
items.answer: We generally advise against ULIPs as a combined investment-insurance vehicle for most clients. Separating insurance (term plan) and investment (mutual funds) typically provides better value, clarity, and flexibility. We will explain why with actual numbers during your consultation — because the answer depends on your specific situation.

items.question: How much term insurance cover do I need?
items.answer: The standard rule of thumb is 10–15x annual income, but this is inadequate for most families. We use an income replacement + liability-adjusted methodology: your sum assured should replace your income for your dependants' needs AND clear all outstanding debts. We calculate this precisely during your insurance needs analysis.

items.question: Is it safe to buy insurance online?
items.answer: Buying online directly from the insurer's website is generally safe and often cheaper (no agent commission in term plans). However, the application form must be filled accurately — particularly health history. Incorrect or incomplete disclosure is the leading cause of claim rejection. We help you fill it right.

items.question: What if I have multiple policies already?
items.answer: Very common, especially in families where different agents sold different policies over the years. We conduct a complete policy audit — listing all existing covers, identifying overlaps, under-insurance, and policies that should be surrendered or continued to maturity. This is offered as a standalone service.

cta.title: Is Your Family Adequately Protected?
cta.subtitle: Book a free insurance needs assessment. We'll tell you exactly where your gaps are — honestly, without any sales pressure.
cta.note: No obligation · Education-first guidance · Asset facilitation

hero.headline: Recover Every Rupee
hero.subtext: Specialist facilitation to reclaim shares and dividends from IEPF, convert physical share certificates to demat, and regularise old family investments. No asset left behind.

problem.heading: Billions in Assets Are Lying Unclaimed Across India
problem.para1: An estimated ₹70,000+ crore is held in the Investor Education and Protection Fund (IEPF) — representing decades of unclaimed dividends and shares from ordinary Indian families who lost track of old investments, changed addresses, or inherited shares without realising it.
problem.para2: Physical share certificates — often stored in trunks or lockers — are valid but increasingly difficult to action without specialist help. Companies have merged, split, changed registrars, and delisted over the years, creating a maze of paperwork.
problem.para3: Our team specialises in navigating exactly this — IEPF claims, physical share dematerialisation, and transmission — so your family doesn't leave what is rightfully yours on the table.

iepfProcess.title: IEPF Claim Process — Step by Step
iepfProcess.subtitle: From discovery to demat credit — we manage every step on your behalf.

steps.title: Discovery & Eligibility Check
steps.description: We search IEPF's database and company records to identify your unclaimed shares and dividends. We verify eligibility and estimate the approximate value before proceeding.

steps.title: Document Collection
steps.description: We prepare a complete checklist of required documents — original share certificates, identity proof, address proof, legal heir documents if applicable, and a demat account statement.

steps.title: IEPF-5 Form Filing
steps.description: We prepare and file Form IEPF-5 with IEPF Authority on your behalf. This is the official government form for claiming shares and dividends transferred to IEPF.

steps.title: Nodal Officer Coordination
steps.description: After IEPF filing, the claim is forwarded to the respective company's Nodal Officer. We track and follow up until the company verifies your claim.

steps.title: Credit to Your Demat Account
steps.description: Once verified by both the company and IEPF Authority, shares are credited to your demat account and unpaid dividends are refunded to your bank account.

physicalShares.heading: Physical Share Certificate Dematerialisation
physicalShares.subheading: Convert your paper share certificates to demat form — safely and completely.

steps.title: Certificate Verification
steps.description: We examine your physical share certificates for validity — check ISIN, distinctive numbers, folio numbers, and company details to confirm legitimacy.

steps.title: Demat Account Setup
steps.description: If you don't have a demat account, we guide you through opening one with a SEBI-registered depository participant. If you have one, we verify it's correctly configured.

steps.title: DRF / Rematerialisation Form
steps.description: Completion of the Dematerialisation Request Form (DRF) with accurate details matched to your certificates. Error-free submission is critical to avoid rejections.

steps.title: Submission to RTA/Depository
steps.description: Physical certificates and forms are submitted to the company's Registrar and Transfer Agent (RTA) via your DP. We coordinate between all parties.

steps.title: Credit Confirmation
steps.description: Shares are credited to your demat account within 15–30 working days subject to RTA processing. We follow up proactively until completion.

transmission.heading: Transmission & Succession Services
transmission.subheading: For families dealing with inherited or jointly held investments.

items.title: Transmission (Death of Holder)
items.description: If a joint holder or sole holder has passed away, shares need to be transmitted to surviving holders or legal heirs. We manage the complete transmission process including affidavit, succession certificate, and RTA coordination.

items.title: Nomination Updates
items.description: Ensuring all your demat and physical holdings have current, valid nominations is critical for smooth succession. We audit and update nominations across all holdings.

items.title: Name/Address Change
items.description: Updating PAN, address, bank mandate, or name across all demat folios and physical share registers — especially common after marriage or address change.

faq.title: IEPF & Recovery — Common Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to the questions families ask us most when dealing with old and unclaimed investments.

items.question: What is IEPF and why were my shares transferred there?
items.answer: IEPF — Investor Education and Protection Fund — is a statutory fund managed by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs. Under the Companies Act 2013, any dividends unclaimed for 7 consecutive years must be transferred to IEPF. Additionally, the corresponding shares are also transferred. This affects millions of Indian investors who lost track of old investments.

items.question: How do I know if my family has shares in IEPF?
items.answer: You can search the IEPF Authority's website (iepf.gov.in) using the shareholder's name or folio number. However, the database can be incomplete or have name variations. We offer a comprehensive search service that cross-references company records, IEPF data, and depository records to identify all possible unclaimed assets.

items.question: Can legal heirs claim IEPF shares after the original holder has passed?
items.answer: Yes. Legal heirs, nominees, and successors can claim IEPF shares. The process involves additional documentation including a death certificate, legal heir certificate or succession certificate, and a will (if applicable). This is one of our most common service requests and we have handled complex multi-generation transmission cases.

items.question: How long does the IEPF claim process take?
items.answer: The official timeline from IEPF Authority is 60–90 days from submission of a complete application. In practice, it often takes 3–6 months due to company verification queues. Transmission cases or large claims involving legal heir documentation can take longer. We track each case and follow up regularly.

items.question: Can physical share certificates still be dematerialised?
items.answer: Yes. Physical share certificates are valid documents and can be dematerialised. Since April 2019, demat is mandated for transfers of listed company shares. Dematerialisation is therefore essential if you want to sell, gift, or pledge your shares. We handle the complete DRF (Demat Request Form) submission process.

items.question: What are your fees for IEPF claim assistance?
items.answer: We charge a transparent facilitation fee — either a flat fee per claim or a percentage of the recovered value, agreed upfront. There are no hidden charges. We will quote our fee before beginning any work and the fee is payable only after successful credit of shares to your demat account (outcome-based model for most cases).

cta.title: Don't Leave Family Assets Unclaimed
cta.subtitle: Our recovery specialists will search, verify, and reclaim what belongs to your family. Book a free discovery consultation to get started.
cta.note: Outcome-based fee model available · No upfront charges for discovery

hero.headline: Built for Indian Families.
hero.subtext: We founded Saarthi Finance because we saw how the financial services industry was structured around product sales rather than client outcomes. We built something different — a fee-only, regulation-first advisory firm that treats every family's financial journey as our responsibility.

mission.heading: Democratising Quality Financial Advice
mission.para1: In India, high-quality, unbiased financial advice has historically been accessible only to the wealthy — through private banking relationships and family offices. Middle-income families were left to navigate a landscape designed to sell them products, not serve their interests.
mission.para2: Saarthi Finance was built to change that. We believe every Indian family — regardless of wealth level — deserves structured, transparent, and genuinely conflict-free financial guidance.
mission.para3: The word "Saarthi" means charioteer in Sanskrit — one who guides, protects, and navigates. It captures exactly how we see our role: not to make decisions for you, but to give you the knowledge and structure to make the right ones yourself.

values.heading: Our Core Values
values.subheading: Not marketing language — these are the principles that determine how we operate every single day.

items.title: Fiduciary First
items.description: We are legally and ethically bound to act in your interest. Fee-only advisory means we earn from you — not from the products we recommend. There is no ambiguity about where our loyalty lies.

items.title: Education Before Action
items.description: We believe informed clients make better decisions and maintain them through market cycles. Every recommendation comes with a thorough explanation — because your understanding is part of the service.

items.title: Family as the Unit
items.description: Financial planning isn't just about an individual's portfolio. It's about the whole family — spouses, children, ageing parents, inheritance, and succession. We plan accordingly.

items.title: Radical Transparency
items.description: Fees are disclosed upfront. Recommendations include reasoning. Limitations are stated openly. We will tell you when something isn't in our scope — and refer you appropriately when needed.

items.title: Regulatory Integrity
items.description: We operate fully within SEBI, IRDAI, and AMFI regulatory frameworks. Compliance isn't a box we tick — it's a core part of how we build trust with every client.

items.title: Long-Term Orientation
items.description: We don't optimise for the next quarter. We build long-term relationships, plan for decade-long goals, and provide the kind of steady, patient guidance that compounds over time.

team.heading: The Team
team.subheading: Regulated professionals. Genuine people. Committed to your family's financial wellbeing.

members.name: Arun Sharma
members.bio: 15+ years in financial services. Former wealth manager at a leading private bank. Founded Saarthi Finance to build a genuinely conflict-free advisory firm for Indian middle-class families.

members.name: Priya Mehta
members.bio: 10 years of goal-based financial planning experience. Specialises in family financial planning, child education planning, and retirement corpus building.

members.name: Vikram Nair
members.bio: Company Secretary with deep expertise in IEPF claims, share transmission, and physical share dematerialisation. Has handled 200+ recovery cases across 50+ companies.

hero.headline: Financial Knowledge
hero.subtext: Free, jargon-free articles and guides written specifically for the Indian financial context. No product pitches. Just clear, actionable information.

featured.heading: How to Claim Your Family's Shares from IEPF — A Complete 2024 Guide
featured.excerpt: Over ₹70,000 crore sits unclaimed in IEPF. If your family has old investments that stopped paying dividends — or you've inherited shares — this comprehensive guide walks you through every step of the recovery process, documents required, and timelines.

items.title: How to Claim Your Family's Shares from IEPF — A Step-by-Step Guide
items.excerpt: Millions of rupees in unclaimed dividends and shares sit with the Investor Education and Protection Fund. If your family has shares that stopped receiving dividends, they may have been transferred to IEPF. Here's how to reclaim them.

items.title: Why Every Indian Family Needs Term Insurance — And How to Pick the Right One
items.excerpt: Term insurance is the simplest, most affordable financial protection you can give your family. But choosing the wrong cover amount, tenure, or insurer can leave gaps. Here's what matters.

items.title: Dematerialising Physical Share Certificates: What Families Need to Know
items.excerpt: Physical share certificates are valid but increasingly difficult to trade or transfer. SEBI has mandated demat for new transfers. Here's a clear guide to converting your old paper certificates.

items.title: Goal-Based Investing: Why Your Portfolio Should Have a Purpose
items.excerpt: Investing without a goal is like driving without a destination. Here's how to build an investment portfolio around your actual life goals — child's education, retirement, home purchase.

items.title: 5 Common SIP Myths That Could Cost Indian Families Money
items.excerpt: SIPs are powerful tools, but misconceptions about how they work lead to poor decisions. We address the five most common myths with clear explanations.

items.title: Building Your Emergency Fund: How Much is Enough?
items.excerpt: An emergency fund is the most important financial tool most families overlook. Learn how to size yours correctly and where to keep it for safety and accessibility.

items.title: Health Insurance in India: A Complete Family Guide for 2024
items.excerpt: From family floater plans to top-up covers, from pre-existing conditions to portability — a comprehensive guide to choosing health insurance that actually works when you need it.

items.title: ELSS vs PPF: Which Should You Choose for Section 80C?
items.excerpt: Both are popular 80C instruments, but they serve different purposes. A balanced comparison on liquidity, returns, risk, and when each makes sense in a financial plan.

items.title: Why Nominations are Not the Same as a Will — And Why Both Matter
items.excerpt: Most people assume a nominee automatically inherits assets. That's not entirely true. Understanding the distinction between nominations and succession law can prevent family disputes.

newsletter.heading: Get New Guides in Your Inbox
newsletter.subtext: New articles on financial planning, tax updates, and regulatory changes — sent monthly. No spam. Unsubscribe anytime.
newsletter.privacyNote: We respect your privacy. Your email is never shared.
